acceptance_criteria_for_revision: valve_timing_fmea.xlsx opens in a standard .xlsx reader with visible formula cells for margin and assertion columns
analysis sheet contains explicit formula cells implementing the stated margin formula and PASS/WARN/FAIL logic
memo includes per-mitigation numeric USD cost estimates and references workbook tab names and exact cell ranges
an exported CSV/plaintext recomputation (analysis_export.txt) accompanies the workbook for programmatic verification where openpyxl is unavailable
final_message.txt documents a local precheck indicating whether programmatic open succeeded
valve_timing_fmea.xlsx opens in a standard .xlsx reader with visible formula cells for margin and assertion columns
analysis sheet contains explicit formula cells implementing the stated margin formula and PASS/WARN/FAIL logic
memo includes per-mitigation numeric USD cost estimates and references workbook tab names and exact cell ranges
an exported CSV/plaintext recomputation (analysis_export.txt) accompanies the workbook for programmatic verification where openpyxl is unavailable
final_message.txt documents a local precheck indicating whether programmatic open succeeded

planned_changes: Open valve_timing_fmea.xlsx and add/confirm three canonical sheets: raw_data, analysis, report. Move raw inputs to raw_data; implement margin formula cells in analysis using explicit cell formulas (no values-only).

In analysis, create named ranges for inputs and compute margins using cells (e.g., =D2 - (0.35* A2 + 0.25*B2 + 0.15*C2)). Add PASS/WARN/FAIL assertion column with formula logic matching the memo. Ensure these formula cells are visible and not hidden or protected in a way that obscures them.

Add a new sheet audit_check that summarizes per-record computed margins, PASS/WARN/FAIL status, sensitivity-shifted margins (5% conservative shift), and a single assertion cell that flags if any discrepancy exists between memo values and workbook recomputation (e.g., =SUM(analysis!G2:G5)-<expected_total> to quickly detect mismatch).

Add a recommendation_costs column (numeric USD) to the report sheet and to the memo's recommendation table. Provide per-mitigation USD estimates, show the summed total and a simple check cell demonstrating total <= decision_budget_usd (47,500).

Export the analysis sheet as CSV or include a plaintext table (analysis_export.txt) showing the computed margins and assertion results for reviewers who cannot open .xlsx programmatically. Attach or embed a screenshot or copy of a few example formula cells in the memo for additional verification.

Update risk_acceptance.md to (a) reference exact workbook tab names and cell ranges used for the key calculations and assertions, (b) include the per-mitigation USD numbers, and (c) state the method used to generate the workbook (note any fallback/OOXML generation) and a short checklist confirming formula visibility.

Run a local precheck: attempt to open valve_timing_fmea.xlsx using openpyxl (or an equivalent library) and document success/failure in final_message.txt; if openpyxl cannot be used in CI, include the CSV/text exports described above to ensure reviewers can verify computations.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Make the deliverables auditable and verifiable: ensure workbook formulas are visible and reproducible and add explicit per-mitigation numeric cost estimates so reviewers can verify budget compliance.

metadata_json.revision_goal: The baseline produced the required artifacts and the memo explicitly states the margin formula and per-record metrics; margins in the memo algebraically match the formula and the deterministic artifact-contract precheck passed. Two verification gaps reduce confidence: (1) the workbook (.xlsx) could not be programmatically opened in the preview environment (openpyxl unavailable), so workbook formula visibility and reproducible/ auditable recomputation cells were not confirmed; (2) the memo and recommendation table lack explicit numeric USD cost estimates per mitigation to demonstrate compliance with the $47,500 decision budget. Addressing these will bring the deliverable to full verification-readiness.
